summary: Switch check/gate/explain to read nested-member facts from the new lock rows; retire CustomKind::fold_members's fence re-parse with no fallback, per 0018

edit.description: Retire `CustomKind::fold_members` (currently folds `features.nested_members` from re-parsed `features.fenced_blocks`) and its call from `CustomKind::extract`; source `nested_members` from the new `NESTED-MEMBER-LOCK-ROW` lock rows instead. Rework or drop `overlay_templates`, whose purpose was priming the `Primitive::Fenced` substrate `fold_members` folded against — that substrate has no remaining consumer once the fold retires.

`features()` (the sole choke point every custom/built-in member's `Features` is built through) switches its embedded-member fact source from `kind.extract(unit)`'s fold output to the member's `NESTED-MEMBER-LOCK-ROW` rows.

`Features::nested_members`/`embedded_leaves` are populated from lock rows rather than `parse_embedded_info`/`parse_embedded_member`'s TOML re-parse; retire those two parse helpers once no caller survives (verify via `rg` before deleting — `Primitive::Fenced`/`body_fenced_blocks` itself may still be a legitimate raw-text primitive for other uses).

`kind_features`, `builtin_features_by_kind`, `explain`, and `collect_directive_members` all route through `builtin_kind::features()`; confirm each still resolves nested-member facts correctly now that the choke point's fact source changed.
Rewrite the fold-through-fence assertions (fence parse, leaf/child addressing, undeclared-kind/`genre.`-prefix no-ops, the emit↔read round trip) to assert the lock-row-driven path instead.

`a_host_kinds_declared_templates_round_trip_through_the_lock` and `a_lock_declared_templates_row_folds_a_builtin_hosts_member_fence` currently assert fold-through-`explain` behavior; rework onto the new read path.

Review the `fenced_blocks`/`member.manifest`-info-string assertions (~lines 79-136): keep raw-fence-primitive coverage if `Primitive::Fenced` survives for another purpose, drop any nested-member-fold assumption.

Fixture construction (~lines 37-38, 190) hand-builds `Features` with `nested_members`/`EmbeddedMember` literals to test `impact`/`context`; switch the fixture source to whatever the new lock-row read path produces so the fixtures stay representative.

tests.asserts: check/gate/explain resolve an embedded member's facts from its lock row, never by re-parsing the rendered TOML fence.
The two fold-through-explain tests pass against the lock-row read path with no fence re-parse involved.

acceptance: `CustomKind::fold_members` and its fence-re-parse dependency are retired with no fallback (0018: 'no caller survives it'); check/gate/explain/impact/context all resolve nested-member facts from declared lock rows; the full test suite (including tests/extract_equivalence.rs, tests/read_verbs.rs) is green.
notes: Slice 2 of 2 for decision 0018; scoped at 3ed138f. This is the one atomic swap — the choke point (`builtin_kind::features`) and every downstream caller must move together, since a partial switch would leave some paths reading facts the other paths no longer trust. 0018 explicitly rejected keeping the fence as a lenient second read face.

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper`, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`, still at 0.0.5 pending publish — SDK-VERSION-LOCKSTEP); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped. Re-verified at c93eeed (ship audit): still no `.github/workflows/release.yml` (only `temper.yml`, a check job); root package.json is still the private flume manifest (`temper-flume-harness`).

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (distribution.md).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, distribution.md). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (distribution.md 'Fail-loud invariant').
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm `@dtmd/temper`; ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. install.rs range-correctness shipped in SCAFFOLD-OUTPUT-VALID (^0.0.4); release owns only future re-pins.
